recommendations: add a minimal MCP smoke test suite (server starts
each endpoint imports
one happy-path call per core endpoint)
tighten endpoint naming consistency to a single style (e.g.
snake_case only) and remove duplicates like UnitSystem/unitsystem
generate JSON schemas and richer docstrings for every endpoint input/output (especially astropy units/quantities) to reduce misuse
add robust error mapping in adapter layer (ValueError/TypeError/ImportError → structured MCP errors with actionable messages)
pin and validate packaging metadata discovery (current analysis says pyproject/setup not detected despite files existing) to avoid brittle import/deploy behavior
introduce CI for MCP plugin separately from upstream tests (lint
type-check
smoke tests
dependency install matrix)
add compatibility guards and explicit optional-dependency checks for galpy/agama/h5py/pyyaml with clear fallback responses
create a small set of deterministic numerical regression tests for key physics workflows (orbit integration
potential evaluation
unit conversions) exposed via MCP
add request-level validation constraints (array sizes
finite values
unit compatibility) before calling gala internals
implement lightweight caching for repeated static model construction (e.g.
common built-in potentials) to reduce endpoint latency
improve observability with structured logging + per-endpoint timing/error counters
document version compatibility policy (Python/numpy/scipy/astropy + gala release range) in README_MCP and enforce at startup
add security hardening for MCP execution (input size limits
timeout controls
safe temp-file handling for load/save)
provide canonical end-to-end examples in README_MCP (create potential → integrate orbit → serialize)
and add benchmark checks for high-cost endpoints to catch performance regressions early

success_factors: Workflow reached terminal success state and executed all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Resilience mechanism worked: after git clone TLS failure, pipeline continued via zip/import fallback and still produced MCP artifacts.
Import-mode adapter strategy matched repository characteristics (high import feasibility 0.93, low intrusiveness risk).
Generated MCP service started healthy over stdio, indicating baseline runtime viability.
failure_reasons: Primary source acquisition failed due to network/TLS issue (LibreSSL SSL_ERROR_SYSCALL to github.com:443).
Metadata detection inconsistency: analysis reported no pyproject/setup despite files present, reducing confidence in dependency/environment inference.
Validation depth is weak: original project tests not executed (reported failed with empty details) and MCP test coverage effectively smoke-level.

node_performance.download_time: Download node encountered clone failure early; fallback path prevented hard stop. Time likely dominated by failed network handshake/retry behavior.
node_performance.analysis_time: Analysis completed quickly relative to repository size using fallback-ingested tree (188 files), but with shallow/partially inconsistent metadata extraction.
node_performance.generation_time: Generation was efficient (small output set: 7 files) and completed successfully; endpoint surface was created from identified core modules/classes.
node_performance.test_time: Runtime validation appears minimal (service health/startup checks). No substantive functional endpoint tests or upstream test execution.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Undetermined from metrics (reported 0), but observed workload suggests low-to-moderate memory profile for generation/smoke run.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Undetermined from metrics (reported 0); pipeline likely CPU-light except for static analysis.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Low generated artifact footprint (7 files), though reported size=0 indicates missing instrumentation rather than true zero usage.
